title: Flaky Chinese Sea Bass Crumble
description: A flaky and flavorful brunch-mood dish featuring sea bass with toast and a high-protein-friendly crumble topping.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: light soy sauce for a lighter flavor

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: extra virgin or regular depending on taste preference

ingredients.name: Rice Crackers
ingredients.notes: toasted and broken into small pieces for crumble topping

instructions: Toast bread until golden brown and crisp. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Crush the cooled bread with a rolling pin or in a food processor into crumb-sized pieces, about 2 tablespoons at a time for each crumble layer.
In a mixing bowl, combine soy sauce, garlic powder, olive oil, and rice crackers to make a crumb mixture. Mix well until no big chunks remain.

Arrange sea bass fillets in an oven-safe dish with the crumb mixture spread evenly over them. Bake for 10 minutes per inch of thickness or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
While the fish bakes, prepare the side: toast remaining bread pieces into smaller pieces. Spread on a plate and top with crumbled sea bass pieces to make it look appealing.
Serve hot as part of a brunch meal. Garnish with finely chopped green onions for an extra pop of color and flavor.
